an1.76:1.1: “Next to nothing, monks, is loss of relatives.
an1.76:1.2: This is the worst loss: loss of discernment.”
an1.77:1.1: “Next to nothing, monks, is increase in relatives.
an1.77:1.2: This is the foremost increase: increase in discernment.
an1.77:1.3: Therefore, monks, you should train yourselves:
an1.77:1.4: ‘We will grow in the increase of discernment.’
an1.77:1.5: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
an1.78:1.1: “Next to nothing, monks, is loss of wealth.
an1.78:1.2: This is the worst loss: loss of discernment.”
an1.79:1.1: “Next to nothing, monks, is increase in wealth.
an1.79:1.2: This is the foremost increase: increase in discernment.
an1.79:1.3: Therefore, monks, you should train yourselves:
an1.79:1.4: ‘We will grow in the increase of discernment.’
an1.79:1.5: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
